title: actor document publicKey.owner check uses actor_uri not keyId actor
description: In actor_cache.rs get_public_key(), the cache is keyed by actor_uri (the actor URL without the fragment). The code fetches actor_uri, extracts publicKey.owner, and checks owner == actor_uri. However, the keyId in the Signature header has its fragment stripped (key_id.split('#').next()) to produce actor_uri before the cache lookup. If a remote server hosts multiple actors and their key documents cross-reference each other (misconfiguration), the owner check uses the stripped actor URI, not the keyId that was originally requested, which could allow a key from actor A to be accepted as belonging to actor B if both share the same actor document URL base.
close_reason: False positive: publicKey.owner check already compares against the fetched URL.
